title: Starting with an initial capital of $180,000 what steady, disciplined investing can look like over time.
author: Independent_Gur8648
selftext:
Since achieving success following a period of significant losses, I have consistently maintained ample cash reserves in my account for the purpose of risk management. Most people assume that the larger an account grows, the greater the associated risk. For me, however, the reality has proven to be quite the opposite. Once my account size crossed a certain specific threshold, I realized that I could no longer trade in the same manner as I had in the past. The trading habits that had proven effective during the "small account" phase suddenly began to feel... fraught with danger.

The bad habits I used to fall prey to included: habitually waiting too long for momentum to build before rushing into a trade; blindly adding to a position to "average down" and offset a poor entry point; overtrading during choppy, consolidating market days; and prematurely scaling up a position before the trading setup had been fully confirmed.

These practices did occasionally pay off but just as often, they would wipe out weeks' worth of accumulated profits in the span of a single day.

Consequently, I decided to radically simplify everything.

Today, my trading principles essentially boil down to the following points.

1. Patiently wait for the trend to become clear (I no longer attempt to predict market movements).

2. Do not rely solely on price signals; instead, use trading volume to confirm the validity of a signal.

3. Keep initial position sizes smaller than what my intuition might suggest.

4. Only scale up a position when my initial judgment proves correct; never add to a position when I am proven wrong;

5. Cut losses decisively, and never harbor the wishful and dangerous illusion that "the market will eventually bounce back."

No single strategy can guarantee a win every single day, and I still experience losses from time to time. However, the difference now is this: my losses are strictly contained within a manageable range, the returns on my winning trades have become significantly larger, and my overall performance demonstrates a high degree of consistency.

The most profound transformation I have undergone lies not in the specific trading setups or strategies themselves, but rather in my finally accepting a fundamental truth: I do not need to attempt to capture every single fluctuation in the market. Ironically, once I stopped trying to seize every conceivable opportunity the market presented, I actually ended up missing fewer opportunities than before. What most people truly need is not some magical technical indicator; rather, they require strict discipline, ample patience, and a repeatable trading system.

I do not claim that this is the \*only\* "best" way to trade, but it is certainly an effective method I employ to avoid surrendering my hard-won profits back to the market.

I am no guru; I am merely sharing some of my risk mitigation techniques and trading approaches. While they may not be suitable for everyone, if you currently find yourself in a difficult situation struggling with losses and needing to get back on track or if you are a beginner looking to venture into the stock and options markets while mastering effective risk management skills, I would be happy to share with you, free of charge, a "Pre Market Checklist" I recently compiled. It has genuinely helped me maintain consistency in my trading; the checklist contains nothing flashy just a set of trading rules and strategies that I can, and do, strictly adhere to.
